title: Peppery Dense Moroccan Corn Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring white cornmeal, slow-cooked to perfection with a blend of aromatic spices and a kick of pepper, perfect for satisfying midnight cravings. This raw-friendly recipe is also suitable for those looking for a dense and comforting meal.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, water, and a pinch of salt. Let it sit for 10-15 minutes to allow the cornmeal to absorb the water.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven or heavy pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8-10 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, ground cumin, smoked paprika, and black pepper. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes.
Add the cornmeal mixture to the pot and stir to combine with the onion and spice mixture.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 25-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cornmeal is cooked and the mixture is dense and creamy.
Season the tagine with salt to taste.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
Let it cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
Reheat the tagine over low heat, stirring occasionally, until warmed through.
